summary: Machine policy for the Windows Location Provider. Microsoft documents the same device policy path and value name that the app writes, and the policy description matches the disabled behavior.

setting.name: Windows Location Provider
setting.casual_explanation: Windows has a built-in location provider that apps and system features can use. This policy decides whether that provider is available.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable it when they want to reduce location access across the device or enforce a privacy-oriented baseline.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning it off reduces location exposure, but maps, weather, geofencing, device location, and other location-aware features can stop working or work less well.
setting.professional_notes: LocationProviderAdm.admx maps this machine policy to HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\LocationAndSensors\DisableWindowsLocationProvider with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows does not force the machine policy for the Windows Location Provider.

allowed_values.label: Windows Location Provider allowed
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is disabled and the provider remains available.

allowed_values.label: Windows Location Provider disabled
allowed_values.meaning: The provider is blocked by policy.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes DisableWindowsLocationProvider = 1, which matches the documented disable behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows device-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 8 and newer systems with Windows Location Provider policy support

states.rationale: The machine policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: This is a policy default and not a statement about whether location is globally enabled for every user or app.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Devices that use maps, weather, or other location-aware features
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to keep ordinary location-provider behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want the Windows Location Provider disabled across the device.
tradeoffs: Location-aware features can continue to use the Windows provider when otherwise permitted.
No hard privacy restriction is enforced for this provider.

recommended_profiles.label: Privacy focused
intended_for: Managed devices that should not expose Windows location services
avoid_for: Users who rely on location-aware apps and system features

states.rationale: Disable the Windows Location Provider by policy.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows-provided location data unavailable to apps and system features.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You use features that expect device location to work normally.
tradeoffs: Location exposure is reduced at the provider level.
Apps and features that rely on Windows location can lose functionality.

evidence.title: Microsoft Policy CSP: DisableWindowsLocationProvider
evidence.summary: Official documentation for the policy that controls the Windows Location Provider.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft LocationProviderAdm.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file shows DisableWindowsLocationProvider with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0 under the LocationAndSensors policy key.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes DisableWindowsLocationProvider = 1 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak source record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

evidence.title: nohuto win-config mirror - DisableWindowsLocationProvider policy
evidence.summary: LocationProviderAdm.admx shows DisableWindowsLocationProvider under HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\LocationAndSensors. The mirrored ExplainText says enabling the policy turns off the Windows Location Provider feature for the computer, while disabling or not configuring it leaves the feature available to programs. EnabledValue=1 and DisabledValue=0 are explicit.

decision.why: Microsoft documents DisableWindowsLocationProvider directly in the ADMX_LocationProviderAdm Policy CSP and maps it to Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\LocationAndSensors\DisableWindowsLocationProvider. The same page describes that enabling the policy turns off the Windows Location Provider for the computer, and the app writes that exact machine policy surface.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: This policy setting turns off the Windows Location Provider feature for this computer. If you enable this policy setting, the Windows Location Provider feature will be turned off, and all programs on this computer won't be able to use the Windows Location Provider feature. ADMX mapping: Registry Key Name Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\LocationAndSensors; Registry Value Name DisableWindowsLocationProvider.
validation_proof.notes: The ADMX_LocationProviderAdm Policy CSP page explicitly names the exact machine policy path and value name the app writes, and its policy description matches the disabled behavior. Added nohuto mirror corroboration via nohuto-disablewindowslocationprovider-admx.
